title: Today's Tech Brief: Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law
summary: A look at the latest tech news, including AI regulation and Firefox updates.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law
imageCaption: Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law
imageSourceTitle: Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you today. First up, it seems like President Trump is losing some control over AI regulation. A landmark law has been passed in Illinois that's making waves in the tech world.

According to a report from Ars Technica, just a few days after President Trump canceled a plan to regulate AI, Illinois passed a law that's taking a more proactive approach. The details are still emerging, but it's clear that this law is a significant step forward in regulating AI. I'm looking at this as a major shift in how we think about AI regulation, and it's interesting to see states taking the lead on this issue.

In other news, Firefox 151 is out, and it's got some great features. One of the most notable is the ability to edit PDFs right in the browser. No more switching to a separate app or uploading to a cloud service. It's a small thing, but it's one of those quality-of-life improvements that makes you wonder how you ever lived without it. And if you're a Linux user, you'll appreciate that Firefox 151 makes it easy to switch from Windows, exporting your profile, extensions, and themes along the way.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law
Firefox 151 helps you edit PDFs – and switch OSes
